text: Don Felker Welcome to the show.

text: Hey, before we get going, I wanted to let you all know that Kaushik and I are now putting out videos on YouTube.

text: So be sure to check us out on YouTube.

text: I'm talking about anything that's software development related, also introducing anything that's freelancing related.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: With that, let's get to the show.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I just got home from the gym and right before I hopped in the shower, I just kind of had this thing hit me.

text: And it's an observation that an experience I wanted to share with you.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now, this is completely off the cuff.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I didn't plan for this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Inspiration hit me about 45 seconds ago.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I realized after
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: years and years of saying, Hey, that's a good idea.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'll do it later.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That inspiration is perishable.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Um, I got that from Corey house.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: He said it to me over a video call one day and, uh, that was six or seven years ago.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So here we are.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Uh, inspiration is hit and, um,
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: if this message reaches one person out of everyone who listens of thousands of people who usually listen to this podcast, every time we put out an episode, which again, I'm thankful for every single one of you.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Um, but if it just hits one of you and it makes a difference, then, Hey, it did its job.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So that's why I'm putting this out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Um, so what am I here to tell you?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Uh, that you have to keep trying.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's literally at its core basic understanding that you have to keep going.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That as a developer or someone who wants to get into programming, you're going to run into situations where you just feel like the dumbest person in the world.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And as someone with over, it's crazy to say this now, over 20 years of experience in the software industry, I never thought I would say that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm some guy who was a motorcycle mechanic and car mechanic and cabinet maker.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And now I'm a software professional.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And being in this industry for this long is crazy.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And so...
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: For me, I understand this very well.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Like I don't learn things quickly.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: People say, well, you might've written books or you've spoken on stage or anything, but like none of that came easy.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It was all a ton of work and it's still a ton of work.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I just maybe might see patterns now that help me learn things a little quicker, but still, I don't understand a lot of stuff.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's very frustrating for me.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's very difficult for me to sit down and learn a new technology and not know how to do a stupid for loop over an array in a language I've never worked with.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I have 20 years of experience, shouldn't I know how to do this?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But I don't when I start something new.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And the one thing I've realized is that I just can't give up because I know eventually I will win.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it doesn't mean win some huge financial reward, but it means that whatever I'm trying to learn, I'll get through it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'll win in that regard.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And the reason why we're on this podcast right now is because
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Recently, I don't know what it is, but I've had a lot of people reach out to me through Twitter DMs and Instagram DMs and just ask me for advice on, hey, I'm trying to get started in the industry.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Should I go mobile?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Should I go web?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Should I learn AI?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Should I do ML?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Which one's gonna be the best for me?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I don't have the answer to that question.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Then I tell them that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I say, I don't know.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You should just go with something that you find interesting.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then if that changes in six months, that changes in six months.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The breadth of the software industry is so large that you can do anything.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And usually after that question is,
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: well, I'm not sure I'm smart enough to do that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't understand.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I feel really dumb and I can't figure this out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And a lot of times I just want to quit.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Well, here's the secret.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I wanna quit all the time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: This is not easy.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's not easy at all.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And to give you just a quick example, I've been using Tailwind CSS, which is a front end CSS framework for the web for a couple of years now.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And just this morning, I was trying to get a nice little, I had this design in my head of I want on a larger screen, three divs should be equally spaced apart.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But on smaller screens, they should stack.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But inside of each one of those items, certain things should do certain things.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's not complicated.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: This stuff happens all over the web every day.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I was trying to figure out how to do this with Flexbox and using the Flex utilities in Tailwind.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: If you're not familiar with it, it's just CSS classes that are pre-built for you.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And...
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I have stumbled over this all morning long, trying to figure out why it works.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I'm gonna tell you right now, I still don't have to figure it out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm probably, I don't know, 60 to 70% of the way there.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And for someone who is really well-versed in Tailwind CSS, this is probably a 10 minute task for them.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But for me, I'm going on five or six hours minimum of trying to figure this out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now I might have to do this another five or six times.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I might spend 40 plus hours trying to figure this CSS thing out, but going forward, I'll have an example and I'll save it somewhere where I can reference it later.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And what I'm trying to tell you is, is it like none, like none of this comes easy to everybody.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Like even people who've been in the industry who have, who,
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: who write books, who are on podcasts, who teach videos.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I mean, hell, I have a nine hour Kotlin course on how to learn the Kotlin programming language.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And sometimes I forget stuff and I'll have to go Google it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And what pops up?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: One of my videos.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Talk about a humbling experience when you're reminding yourself of how to do something.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But I'm not embarrassed of that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That just is what it is.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I think that's important for everyone to know that you can't expect yourself to be an expert in everything.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I am an expert in very few things.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Some people might consider me an expert in some technologies and even I still might not consider myself an expert in those, whatever those technologies might be.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But the real thing that I want to get across here is that if you're in a situation where you feel like giving up, you feel dumb, you feel to the point of this is too hard for me.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm not smart enough to do this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You are.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You are smart enough.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You just have to just keep pushing.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You're going to be, you know, you're really having to work hard right now.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: This is a struggle.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I know because I've been there and I'm still there.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm there all the time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: None of this is easy.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I work with a lot of really, really intelligent people.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: My current client, people who are next, just light years ahead of everybody.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's a very humbling experience.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: When I have conversations with these people, sometimes I feel like the biggest idiot in the world.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I have a lot of experience.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So it happens to us all.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's just something that's kind of a feeling you have to realize like, all right, I know I don't understand this, but I know I can figure it out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And how do I figure it out?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You have to understand how you learn.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Are you a visual learner?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Are you someone who likes to read and watch a video and then do it?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Are you someone who can just hear it and do it?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Are you someone who can just read a book and implement it?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Everybody's different.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: For me, what I like to do is I like to go read about it a little bit.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Usually through a blog post, I want to know how to do something, the exact thing I want to do.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then I want to watch a video on it and I want to watch, see how it's done.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then I like to follow along in my own project and kind of implement it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then I like to set break points or debuggers and change things and see what happens.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then I like to inspect it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then I kind of start feeling like, all right, now what if I tweak this?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And what if I tweak that?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: What happens?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's how I learn.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: For me, that's how I learn.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's exactly what I did this morning with this Tailwind CSS thing.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I got it working somewhat.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I mean, it looks kind of nice.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It looks a lot better than it did before.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But when I change the screen resolution, it looks like garbage.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It looks terrible.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't know what I need to do to fix it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: What I learned is I need to relearn Tailwind's breakpoints, which are the media breakpoints where the different size screen things, you know, change.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: If it's a small, medium, large, extra large screen, then, you know, different properties can apply.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I need to relearn those.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I learned them a couple of years ago, but I have forgot them.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's, you know.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That is what it is.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But again, the reason why we're here is because I'm getting a lot of these DMs.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm more than normal.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I used to get one every couple of months and now it seems like I'm getting a few a week or multiple a week of like, hey, I don't know this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm not smart enough.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's not just from people in the United States.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: There's people from all over the world, from the United States to Europe, to Africa, to Asia, Australia, all over the world I'm getting these messages.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's what I wanna tell you all, if you're listening here to this short little podcast is that, you know what, you got this, you got it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You just have to trust yourself to keep learning.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: If you can't learn something and it doesn't make sense, you can't make it work the way you think it should work, try a different way.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And maybe what you're going to find is by doing it this different way, you're going to learn like, oh, it exposes you to something you didn't see before.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And you can kind of connect the dots on the other side.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And that's what happens with me a lot of times.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'll learn a bunch about a certain way to do something in technology.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'll want to do it one way, it won't work, and I'll just kind of hack it together another way.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then I'll kind of see like, oh, if I take A and I put B together and I just kind of tweak it, oh, that's how they can connect.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now it makes sense.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I was fighting the framework before.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I need to just kind of go along with the conventions.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But I wouldn't have learned that if I just gave up.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I think a lot of people just feel that they're not smart enough because they don't get it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But...
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You tell me to go, hey, Don, go write some Rust code.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't even want to install it right now.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't know.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Don, go run R. I don't even know what the syntax of R is.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't know.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: There's so much out there.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And so I like to call it, you know, just in time learning.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So, you know, JIL, just in time learning, like kind of missing the T there, but you get what I mean, just in time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So whenever I need it, I learn it then.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I've been using Tailwind for a couple of years now.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And a lot of times I'll just take some snippets from Tailwind UI or something I find online and I can tweak it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But now I need something that's specific and I can't find any examples online.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So I'm having to do it myself.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I know the framework can do it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I just need to learn it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's frustrating.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Um, at times I procrastinate and I don't want to do it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I just want to give up and go do something else.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I want to go for a walk.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I want to go work out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I want to work on a different project.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I want to work, do client work.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I want to play video game.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I want to watch TV, hang out with my kids, play the piano, like anything, but learn this thing because it's so frustrating and difficult because it's just not clicking for me.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So that happens to all of us.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But you just got to keep with it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Keep trying and know that you will get through it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And each time you learn something, it's just going to help you grow a little bit more and a little bit more.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's going to keep building.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's like a snowball.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It really is.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: One thing I learned from years ago from a really smart guy I worked with, as he told me, he's like, you know what?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Try to learn one programming language year.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: One programming language year.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: He's like, you don't have to be a master at it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: You don't have to be a magician at the language, but just learn about it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Write a program in it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: or talk to an API in it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: He goes, learn about the asynchronous nature of that stuff.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: He goes, you're gonna understand and learn so much and you're gonna find out what all of the things in the language do.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then what you're gonna end up finding is how these things relate to other languages.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And then eventually on your second, your third, maybe your fourth language, you're gonna say like, oh, this language does the same thing as this.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I wonder how I can do that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So for example, maybe you're working in Kotlin and you're used to working with the null coalescing operator.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: If you're not sure what that is, take a look.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's a way to handle nulls a little, you know, cleanly.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And you're going to go work with another language and you want to do that inside of JavaScript or Ruby.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But now you can look up that, you know, Ruby coalescing operator.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now you may not know what it is, or it may not even exist for the typical language, but now you at least know what to search for.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And what I'm trying to say is that you're just building upon your knowledge.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So anyway, I can keep going on and on about this, but I'm not.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I need to go take a shower and get ready for some client work.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But if you're in a situation where you're frustrated, you want to give up, you feel like throwing in the towel, you feel like this is not for you, don't do that yet.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I've been there many times.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm there all the time.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I've learned to trust myself that I can get through it and just know that you can do it too.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Just keep trying.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Ask for help online through forums like Stack Overflow.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Find some online chats on various discords of where you can ask questions.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Don't ask people to do the work for you.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Just say, hey, I'm having trouble here.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't understand X.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Can you help me understand it?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: People don't want to do the work for you.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's a quick way to have people not help you out.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But if you're trying and you explain that you're having a difficulty understanding it, people are really apt to help you.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So keep that in mind.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Don't give up.
